# Claw-in-a-Box
> Bounded authorization for AI agents: your agent asks before it spends.

Claw-in-a-Box is an already-live dual-rail x402 service with capability tokens,
spend-policy verdicts, Telegram human approval, persistence, paid agent claiming,
strict identity mode, one-shot execution bindings, and an operator Console.

## Operator Console
- Purpose: operate and inspect the service without moving enforcement into the frontend.
- Access tiers: visitor; agent owner with X-Agent-Secret; operator with a session-only bearer key.
- Console source and v0.8.1+ hardening were built with Codex + GPT-5.6 for OpenAI Build Week.
- The v0.1-v0.8.0 production backend is prior infrastructure, not claimed as Build Week work.

## Canonical resources
- Homepage: https://clawinabox.xyz/
- API health: https://api.clawinabox.xyz/healthz
- Machine/API guide: https://api.clawinabox.xyz/skill.md
- Documentation: https://clawinabox.xyz/documentation.html
- Public repository: https://github.com/ckeda/claw-in-a-box

## Safety and access
- Public NANDA endpoints remain free and backward compatible.
- Approval lists require the operator bearer key.
- Per-agent spend history and owner mutations require that agent's X-Agent-Secret.
- Public metrics are aggregate-only and contain no agent, wallet, destination, or chat identifiers.
- Never send secrets in URLs, logs, or prompts. Paid routes require an explicit x402 payment flow.
